result.author: Office of Bushfire Risk Management
result.name: bush-fire-prone-areas-designated-on-13-12-2025-obrm-025
result.notes:
The Bush Fire Prone Areas designated on 13/12/2025 dataset (OBRM-025) highlights new additions to the bush fire prone areas of Western Australia as designated by the Fire and Emergency Services (FES) Commissioner on 13 December 2025.

This dataset supersedes the Bush Fire Prone Areas 2024 datasets (OBRM-021 and OBRM-022).

For these areas, additional planning and building requirements may apply to development on and from 13 April 2026.

This overlay should be used in conjunction with Bush Fire Prone Areas 2025 (OBRM-024) dataset, which identifies all bush fire prone areas of Western Australia as designated by the Fire and Emergency Services (FES) Commissioner on 13 December 2025.

More information is available from Office of Bushfire Risk Management (OBRM).

Contact: Office of Bushfire Risk Management, obrm@dfes.wa.gov.au

organization.name: department-of-fire-emergency-services
organization.title: Department of Fire and Emergency Services
organization.description: As Western Australia’s leading hazard management agency, the Department of Fire and Emergency Services (DFES) performs a critical role coordinating emergency services for a range of natural disasters and emergency incidents threatening life and property.  DFES works together with the community and government to prevent, prepare for, respond to and recover from a diverse range of emergencies.

result.title: Bush Fire Prone Areas designated on 13-12-2025 (OBRM-025)

groups.description:
_The About page for this group provides data service links and more information regarding this service._ 

This service provides access to bush fire prone related data for public use.

If you have any questions about this service, please contact Landgate Customer Service:

Customer Experience team

[customerexperience@landgate.wa.gov.au](mailto:customerexperience@landgate.wa.gov.au)

+61 (0)8 9273 7683 (direct line)
Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 5pm (AWST)


For data quality queries please refer to the [dataset](https://catalogue.data.wa.gov.au/group/slip-public-bush-fire-prone-areas-service) Contact details.

Services 
=======

Note, the following services require 3rd party software that supports [OGC Standards](http://www.opengeospatial.org/standards) and [Esri services](http://www.esri.com/).

OGC Web Services
------------------------------

__[Web Map Service (WMS)](https://services.slip.wa.gov.au/public/services/SLIP_Public_Services/Bush_Fire_Prone_Areas/MapServer/WMSServer?)__ _Right mouse click to copy and paste the URL to a WMS server connection._ 

__[Web Feature Service (WFS)](https://services.slip.wa.gov.au/public/services/SLIP_Public_Services/Bush_Fire_Prone_Areas_FS/MapServer/WFSServer?)__ _Right mouse click to copy and paste the URL to a WFS server connection._ 

ESRI ArcGIS users
------------------------------

__[Map Service](https://services.slip.wa.gov.au/public/rest/services/SLIP_Public_Services/Bush_Fire_Prone_Areas/MapServer)__ _Right mouse click to copy and paste the URL to an ArcGIS server connection._

resources.description: This resource provides the latest snapshot of the dataset as GeoJSON. GeoJSON can be opened in many spatial and non-spatial software packages. Note: When accessing this resource you will be prompted to signup for a free account.
resources.name: GeoJSON

resources.description: This resource provides the latest snapshot of the dataset as a shapefile. Shapefiles can be opened in many spatial and non-spatial software packages. Note: When accessing this resource you will be prompted to signup for a free SLIP account.

resources.name: Geopackage

resources.description:
Web Feature Service (WFS) provides an interface that allows requests for geographical features across the web using platform independent calls.

WFS allows a user to download and spatially analyse the features within a datset.

This service is aimed at advanced geographical information users, and will require access to geographical information system (GIS) software such as QGIS and ArcGIS.
resources.name: Web Feature Service

resources.description:
An ArcGIS Server Map Service offers access to a geographic dataset - such as a map or imagery - by providing a set of tiled images for use in web-based maps.

Depending on the capabilities enabled, this web service may also allow a client to dynamically draw, search, and query its features.

This service is aimed at advanced geographical information users, and will require access to geographical information system (GIS) software such as ArcGIS and QGIS.
resources.name: ArcGIS Server Map Service

resources.description:
Web Map Service (WMS) is a standard protocol for serving georeferenced map images over the internet that are generated by a map server using data from a GIS database.

WMS allows a user to spatially visualise the dataset, and query individual features, but does not allow downloading the raw data.

This service is aimed at advanced geographical information users, and will require access to geographical information system (GIS) software such as QGIS and ArcGIS.
resources.name: Web Mapping Service
